data.body:
### [7/10] DOSSIER B — Direction arbitration & the baseline lineage: WHY + the digest risk

ORIGIN INCIDENT: commit 16870dbec (2026-06-25) / class REB-532. Before it, _diff_fields did UNCONDITIONAL local-wins -> a Jira-side field edit was reverted to local every pass (verified live: assigning a ticket in Jira reverted to unassigned). Fix = three-way merge with a common ANCESTOR: local==baseline => local unchanged => divergence is Jira-side => suppress outbound, mirror inbound; local!=baseline => local edit => local-wins. The ancestor is the ONLY direction signal (the inbound differ carries none). Absent baseline => local-wins (safe/lossy); corrupt baseline/store => fail CLOSED (an empty store treats every ticket as unbound -> mass-CREATE duplicate Jira issues, binding_store.py:126-148).

SHAPE-TOLERANCE predates the baseline (bug 85a1, code fossils outbound_fields.py:201-261): Jira returns description as ADF dict, assignee as {accountId,displayName,emailAddress}, status/priority as {name} — literal == vs local scalars fired every pass ("21-mutation idempotency churn"). Fixed by normalizing BOTH sides through one path: _extract_jira_field (ADF->adf_to_text, {name}->.name), _assignee_matches (permissive one-of-three), rstrip text. Commit e57df4539 is the NORMALIZATION-ASYMMETRY fossil: reconcile-check compared RAW Jira vs local WITHOUT the differ's normalization -> ~3660 FALSE discrepancies while the differ dispatched ~0. Lesson: normalization must be SYMMETRIC or every verdict silently corrupts.

edge->level + the CAUTIOUS ROLLOUT (epic 3006-e198; ADR 0026): baf7-868d (foundation: pure classify() + per-binding baseline on BindingStore + circuit breaker + census, wired into nothing) -> 7d23-106a (Phase-1 dual-write SHADOW behind baseline_dual_write, logs baseline_shadow_check, consumed by no one) -> a118-fa8e (Phase-3 consumer swap: re-point both _local_matches_prev sites from prev to get_baseline; 30 consecutive divergent=0 passes over ~4h/~854 bindings, then flip; first flag-ON pass 22 mutations byte-identical to pre-flip) -> d6bd-95c9 (retire flags, hardcode always-on; AC6 = decision-pin regression test with verified revert-teeth). Commits c1e494276, 1cb524c1a, b76dd09a1, c4d58a306; Gerrit 434/437/441/659.
WHY the 5 fields = _INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS {title,description,priority,status,assignee}: 3-way merge only matters where BOTH sides can author = exactly the inbound-mirrored set. NUANCE: _BASELINE_FIELDS uses JIRA name "summary" but _INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS uses LOCAL "title" -> the suppression guard NEVER fires for summary/title (a documented pre-existing partial no-op pinned by a118's test; effective suppression = 4 fields).

INVARIANTS (violation reopens REB-532): (1) the ancestor VALUE must exist and be correct; (2) normalization SYMMETRIC (e57df4539); (3) absent=>None=>local-wins, corrupt-store=>abort — note an individually-wrong-but-well-formed baseline is NOT caught, just produces a wrong verdict; (4) baseline advanced only from CONFIRMED in-window bindings, stored RAW (set_baseline filters to 5 fields, no normalization); (5) direction-preservation is a first-class regression cell.

BEARING ON baseline-as-DIGEST: the baseline is read ONLY for equality (classify.direction_is_inbound local==baseline; outbound_fields _local_matches_prev/_jira_matches_prev) — nothing reconstructs/materializes/displays it -> digest NOT architecturally blocked. BUT the compare is NOT "two canonical forms byte-identical"; risks that flip a verdict (=> REB-532 returns): 
- R1 ASSIGNEE (THE KILLER): _assignee_matches is a ONE-TO-MANY set-membership test (local matches ANY of {emailAddress,accountId,displayName}); a single digest encodes ONE identity. baseline=digest(accountId) vs local stored as displayName => current says MATCH, digest says MISMATCH => local-wins reversion. Not trivially avoidable (needs a hot-path identity resolver, the exact thing _assignee_matches avoided). RECOMMENDATION: EXCLUDE assignee from digesting; keep its raw value.
- R2 ADF canonicalization instability (hash the post-adf_to_text+rstrip TEXT, never the dict); R3 rstrip parity; R4 None (no-baseline=>local-wins) vs digest("")-empty must stay distinguishable; R5 unicode NFC/NFD; R6 status/priority hash the .name; R7 no over-truncation (>=128-bit floor).
IF pursued: derive the digest from the EXACT existing normalizers (never hand-roll), extend the direction-preservation property suite FIRST with R1-R5 adversarial shape cases (existing cells use single-form fixtures and would pass while assignee regresses), then gate via dual-write shadow -> N divergent=0 -> flip -> retire (a118/d6bd template).
